compiled_state.title: [blue-team-filter]: adversarial review output written to repo .claude/artifacts/ -> artifact persists in git instead of temp directory
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: Observed in a host project: dso:blue-team-filter created .claude/artifacts/adversarial-review-241f-ce0d.json in the repo working tree instead of the temporary artifacts directory (get_artifacts_dir() -> /tmp/workflow-plugin-<hash>/). This causes the artifact to show up in git status and potentially be committed. Expected: all agent artifacts write to the temp artifacts directory, not to .claude/artifacts/ in the repo. Actual: blue-team-filter wrote to .claude/artifacts/ in the repo root.

comments.body: Investigation (INTERMEDIATE, opus): blue-team-filter sub-agent has no shell access; it cannot resolve $ARTIFACTS_DIR via get_artifacts_dir, so the LLM fabricates a repo-relative path (.claude/artifacts/...) which ends up in git working tree. Fix is LLM-behavioral: orchestrator resolves ARTIFACTS_DIR before dispatch and passes absolute path as task argument. Agent file must be updated to forbid fabricated paths and reference the supplied {artifacts-dir}. Same pattern may exist in red-team-reviewer. ESCALATE: agent file content edit (plugins/dso/agents/blue-team-filter.md) + skill edit (preplanning SKILL.md Step 2); requires user approval.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: USER_APPROVAL (2026-04-20): Approved for LLM-behavioral fix — edit plugins/dso/agents/blue-team-filter.md to forbid fabricated artifact paths + edit plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/SKILL.md Step 2 to resolve and pass ARTIFACTS_DIR. Also audit red-team-reviewer.md for same pattern.
comments.author: Test
